task_completion.description: Agent accessed the CAMEO Chemicals website to search for the solvent candidates.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A web browser is open showing the CAMEO Chemicals website (cameochemicals.noaa.gov) with search results or a chemical datasheet for one of the candidates.

task_completion.description: Agent extracted Specific Gravity, Flash Point, and Boiling Point for Dichloromethane and Chloroform.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor or terminal shows the report draft containing SG, FP, and BP values for Dichloromethane and Chloroform.

task_completion.description: Agent extracted Specific Gravity, Flash Point, and Boiling Point for Carbon Tetrachloride, Trichloroethylene, and 1,2-Dichloroethane.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor or terminal shows the report draft containing SG, FP, and BP values for Carbon Tetrachloride, Trichloroethylene, and 1,2-Dichloroethane.

task_completion.description: The report is formatted exactly as requested with specific headers and dashed lines.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The text file contains the exact headers 'CANDIDATE SUMMARY' and 'FINAL SELECTION', separated by dashed lines ('--------------------------------------------------'), and follows the 'Chemical: SG=[val], FP=[val], BP=[val]' format.

task_completion.description: The agent correctly selected Dichloromethane (or Methylene chloride) as the final solvent with a valid reason.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Under the 'FINAL SELECTION' section, 'Selected Solvent:' is listed as 'Dichloromethane' (or 'Methylene chloride'). The 'Reason:' mentions it has the lowest boiling point while being non-flammable and denser than water.

task_completion.description: The report is saved to the correct file path.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The text editor's title bar, file explorer, or a terminal command shows the file is saved as 'solvent_selection_report.txt' in the '~/Documents/' directory.

integrity.description: The agent actually used a web browser to search CAMEO Chemicals rather than hallucinating the data.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the agent interacting with the CAMEO Chemicals website in a browser, navigating through different chemical datasheets.

integrity.description: The extracted values in the report match the genuine CAMEO Chemicals data.
integrity.visual_evidence: The SG, FP, and BP values in the report should closely match the verified privileged information (e.g., Dichloromethane BP ~104F, 1,2-Dichloroethane FP ~56F), not placeholder or fabricated numbers.

integrity.description: The agent did not bypass the research step by directly echoing a pre-written answer into the file.
integrity.visual_evidence: There should be evidence of a step-by-step process: browsing the web, gathering data, and then writing to the file, rather than a single terminal command creating the entire completed file instantly.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Verified data from CAMEO Chemicals: 1) Dichloromethane: SG ~1.32, BP ~104°F, Non-flammable. 2) Chloroform: SG ~1.48, BP ~143°F, Non-flammable. 3) Carbon Tetrachloride: SG ~1.59, BP ~170°F, Non-flammable. 4) Trichloroethylene: SG ~1.46, BP ~189°F, Non-flammable. 5) 1,2-Dichloroethane: SG ~1.25, BP ~182°F, Flammable (FP 56°F). The correct selection is Dichloromethane (or its alternative name Methylene chloride) because it is the only candidate that meets the safety criteria (SG > 1.0 and non-flammable) while having the lowest boiling point (103.6°F).
